ops.text:
DELONG MACHINERY VALIDATED (independent, $0, no model load) — before committing the ~20min full-frame run.

Ran fullframe.py's fast_delong/paired_delong on E22's already-persisted per-row probs (628-row watcher frame, base vs retrieval arm) and checked against known ground truth:
- DeLong auc_base = 0.931689674494 == sentinel_auc(labels, fire_base) EXACTLY (|diff|=0.0e+00) == card stored AUC 0.9316896744944222. DeLong AUC == Mann-Whitney/sklearn AUC == stored meta.auc.
- DeLong SE_base = 0.009982 reproduces the instrument card's single-frame DeLong SE (0.00998, card table 2 xchk).
- On the real E22 pair: measured rho=0.8548, SE_delta_paired=0.005578, paired MDE=0.015628 (consistent with card refs 0.0143@rho0.9), delta=-0.005578 == E22 stored delta -0.005577912485 exactly, CI95=[-0.01651,+0.00536].

Conclusion: paired fast-DeLong (Sun & Xu 2014) impl in fullframe.py is correct; SE_base reproduces the card's frame SE; AUC path is byte-exact vs stored. Also confirmed EvalFrame.load builds from watcher_eval.parquet (628 rows) and fullframe.py digest-guards against the stored dataset_digest. Cleared to run full-frame once golden screen picks the config.
